clusters.headline: Pakistan army chief arrives in Switzerland for US-Iran talks
clusters.summary:
Pakistan army chief arrives in Switzerland for US-Iran talks

    
    

        
    
                Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir has arrived in Switzerland to take part in technical-level talks between the United States and Iran under the framework of the Islama

clusters.headline: Fans praise Japan's team spirit as Samurai Blue thrash Tunisia 4-0 and make World Cup history
clusters.summary: Japan are the first Asian team to score four goals at the World Cup.

clusters.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz shut as U.S.-Iran talks set for Sunday in Switzerland
clusters.summary: U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.

clusters.headline: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
clusters.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

clusters.headline: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
clusters.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

clusters.headline: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
clusters.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

clusters.headline: Africa: Rwanda and South Africa - From Diplomatic Frost to Strategic Partnership and Greater People-to-People Mobility
clusters.summary: [allAfrica] After more than a decade of diplomatic estrangement, Rwanda and South Africa appear to be taking meaningful steps toward rebuilding a relationship long marked by suspicion, political tensions, and missed opportunities. Recent high-level engagements between Kigali and Pretoria suggest a g

clusters.headline: Kenya: Fabricated Quote Used to Claim Trump Warned Kenyan President Ruto Over 'Oppressing Kenyans' At G7 Summit
clusters.summary: [Africa Check] Fabricated quote used to claim Trump warned Kenyan president Ruto over 'oppressing Kenyans' at G7 summit

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Nigeria's Ruling Party Has Not Released Final List of Senatorial Candidates for 2027 Elections
clusters.summary: [Africa Check] Nigeria's ruling party has not released final list of senatorial candidates for 2027 elections

clusters.headline: India tightens security as millions of medical student aspirants retake entrance exam after paper leak
clusters.summary: India’s 2.2 million medical aspirants are retaking the entrance exam under heavy security after a paper leak sparked outrage, arrests and protests over exam fraud in the country.

clusters.headline: Rwanda: Genocide Survivors Urge Australia's Griffith University to Disassociate With Judi Rever
clusters.summary: [New Times] Genocide survivors' organisations from Rwanda and across the diaspora have called on Australia's Griffith University to sever any association with Judi Rever, a Canadian author and journalist known for promoting narratives that distort and deny the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi.

clusters.headline: 'It was either killed or be killed' - ongoing nightmares of an ex-child soldier in Somalia
clusters.summary: Yusuf Ali, now 34, struggles with his memories living in Mogadishu surrounded by dark reminders of his past.
